title: How to scale AI and avoid being stuck in the pilot stage - LinkedIn
search_path_label: A 级媒体 / GDELT 路径
full_text:
It was a pleasure speaking to the Wall Street Journal's Steve Rosenbush about one of my favorite topics: how companies can scale AI and derive value from their investments.
It was a pleasure speaking to the Wall Street Journal's Steve Rosenbush about one of my favorite topics: how companies can scale AI and derive value from their investments.
I speak to so many leaders who are missing key components like sponsorship, governance, the right business case, accountability, metrics, and the full funding they need to succeed or adapt to how fast AI is changing. The vast majority of companies - nearly half - report that they are stuck in the pilot stage, meaning they won't reach productivity gains.
How we built in 2023 isn't how we should build in 2025 because innovation is moving so quickly. Think about how your business can remain agile and open to change as we see new AI technology emerge every day.
Move beyond the basics. They are a good entryway but consider AI use cases that change productivity in major workflows. For example, if the finance department thought about new projects as AI-native, what would productivity in fraud detection look like? That's a more audacious and transformative use case to explore.
For more, check out Steve's column here and let me know what you think: https://lnkd.in/gbedpQKC
Companies Are Struggling to Drive a Return on AI. It Doesn’t Have to Be That Way.
